title: I would like to notify a user after a specific number of rows have changed their status
excerpt: I would only like the notification to occur once all rows that been changed to the specific status are marked. For example, only after I mark this third row “Asset/Input Pending”, I’d like it to email xyz. Not after the …

posts.author: HannahTrousdale (Hannah)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would only like the notification to occur once all rows that been changed to the specific status are marked. For example, only after I mark this third row “Asset/Input Pending”, I’d like it to email xyz. Not after the first row/trigger. I’m wondering if their needs to be a final trigger that lets the system know all rows marked “Asset/Input Pending” should create a notification. Attachment below.

image1224×433 48.7 KB

posts.author: mia-cxmonday (Mia Leath)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Hannah!

Mia here from monday.com’s support team I hope you’re doing well! I’d be happy to help you find a workaround for this use case. Can you please let me know what’s your plan tier? That’ll help me see exactly which features are available to you.

I’ll be looking forward to your reply!

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
You might try having each campaign be an item and then the tasks be subitems? THere are more automations around ‘if all subitems are X, then Y’

Desiree - www.thecleverclovers.com
